export const meta = {
  title: "Background Repeat",
  description: "Controls the repetition of the background image of an element.",
};

<ApiTable category="background" name="background-repeat" />

## Repeat

> Initial value

The background image will be repeated both horizontally and vertically.

{/* biome-ignore */}
```html live "br-r" layout="/src/layouts/inlineLightOnly.astro"
<div class="br-r bp-c h-s" style="background-image: url('data:image/svg+xml;base64,');">
  <div class="p-10"></div>
</div>
```

## No Repeat

The background image will not be repeated.

{/* biome-ignore */}
```html live "br-nr" layout="/src/layouts/inlineLightOnly.astro"
<div class="br-nr bp-c h-s" style="background-image: url('data:image/svg+xml;base64,');">
  <div class="p-10"></div>
</div>
```

## Round

The background image will be repeated and resized to fit the area.

{/* biome-ignore */}
```html live "br-ro" layout="/src/layouts/inlineLightOnly.astro"
<div class="br-ro bp-c h-s" style="background-image: url('data:image/svg+xml;base64,');">
  <div class="p-10"></div>
</div>
```

## Repeat X

The background image will be repeated horizontally only.

{/* biome-ignore */}
```html live "br-rx" layout="/src/layouts/inlineLightOnly.astro"
<div class="br-rx bp-c h-s" style="background-image: url('data:image/svg+xml;base64,');">
  <div class="p-10"></div>
</div>
```

## Repeat Y

The background image will be repeated vertically only.

{/* biome-ignore */}
```html live "br-ry" layout="/src/layouts/inlineLightOnly.astro"
<div class="br-ry bp-c h-s" style="background-image: url('data:image/svg+xml;base64,');">
  <div class="p-10"></div>
</div>
```

## Space

The background image will be repeated and spaced evenly.

{/* biome-ignore */}
```html live "br-s" layout="/src/layouts/inlineLightOnly.astro"
<div class="br-s bp-c h-s" style="background-image: url('data:image/svg+xml;base64,');">
  <div class="p-10"></div>
</div>
```

## Using utility variants

Learn how to override existing utilities based on the user's [screen size](/docs/media-queries) or other factors, such as [hover states](/docs/pseudo-classes).

### Using media queries

<MediaVariant class="br-r" variant="br-nr" classPrefix="br">
  ### Breakpoint variant
</MediaVariant>

### Using hover states

<HoverVariant class="br-r" variant="br-nr" classPrefix="br">
  ### Hover variant
</HoverVariant>
